channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tanzania's government outlines measures to stabilize maize prices through NFRA purchases
item.description: The Tanzanian government has announced plans to address declining maize prices by launching official purchases through the National Food Reserve Agency (NFRA). Farmers are facing severe financial chal...

item.title: Mercy Eke's Life Updates: Challenges, Relationships, and Future Plans
item.description: Mercy Eke, the former Big Brother Naija winner, has shared her life updates after her second stint on the reality show. She discussed the challenges she faced while pursuing her master's degree in Bus...

item.title: Top Pool Vacuums for Efficient Maintenance
item.description: Maintaining a pristine pool requires effective cleaning tools, and several options are available to simplify the process. Manual vacuums like the Sepetrel Pool Vacuum Head with Side Brushes ($69.99) o...

item.title: CBSE Class 10 results show 96.78% pass rate with student performance improvements
item.description: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) declared the results of the Class 10 Second Board Examination 2026, marking a significant rise in the overall pass percentage to 96.78%. This increase c...

item.title: Ricky Gervais Defends Free Speech and Controversial Comedy
item.description: Ricky Gervais has reiterated his stance against calls to modify his provocative comedy, emphasizing that free speech should not be curtailed. The comedian, known for his sharp wit and controversial ta...

item.title: Junior Springboks secure back-to-back World Rugby U20 Championship titles after defeating France
item.description: The Junior Springboks retained their World Rugby U20 Championship title with a hard-fought 16-5 victory over France in Tbilisi, Georgia, on Saturday. A 45-minute lightning delay during the final could...

item.title: New York Mayor Considers Arresting Netanyahu During U.N. Visit
item.description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani has indicated that his administration is actively discussing whether to detain Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu during his upcoming visit to the city in Septembe...

item.title: England and France face off in 2026 World Cup bronze final after semi-final exits
item.description: The article details the anticipated clash between England and France in the 2026 FIFA World Cup bronze medal match, following their respective exits from the semi-finals. After a dramatic semi-final d...

item.title: Two Suspects in Kent Carpenter's Murder Face Charges
item.description: Philippine police have filed robbery and homicide charges against two suspects in the killing of American marine biologist Kent Edward Carpenter. Mark Bucog, 26, and Jal Filipinas, 40, were arrested i...

item.title: Manika Batra Overcomes Pressure with Positive Mindset in Table Tennis Comeback
item.description: Manika Batra, India's top female table tennis player, showcased her resilience and positive mindset after a challenging start to the Ultimate Table Tennis (UTT) Season 7. Following three consecutive l...

item.title: Fremantle Dockers dominate Port Adelaide Power with third-quarter surge to secure top-two spot
item.description: Fremantle Dockers delivered a stunning third-quarter performance to dismantle Port Adelaide Power, securing a 28-point victory that solidifies their position in the top two for the season. Despite Por...

item.title: Cash-in-transit vehicle destroyed in Durban robbery
item.description: Emergency services are investigating a violent crime scene on the N3 near Sherwood offramp after suspects attacked a cash-in-transit vehicle. Police confirmed that armed robbers intercepted the van, s...

item.title: US issues Level 4 travel advisory for 23 countries, including Nigeria regions
item.description: The US Department of State has updated its global travel advisory, placing 23 countries under its highest warning level (Level 4: Do Not Travel) due to severe security risks and limited consular assis...

item.title: Premier Doug Ford Inspects Thunder Bay Amid Wildfire Evacuations
item.description: Premier Doug Ford visited Thunder Bay, Ontario, to assess the situation as wildfires in northern Ontario continue to force evacuations. The Canadian Press report highlights that many evacuees have sou...

item.title: LAPD Detective Alleges Retaliation After Investigating Suspicious Death
item.description: An LAPD detective has filed a lawsuit against the Los Angeles Police Department, alleging he faced retaliation for investigating the death of Amelia Salehpour. The case centers on conflicting accounts...

item.title: Trump administration weakens endangered species protections with policy changes
item.description: The Trump administration has implemented two new policies that weaken the Endangered Species Act (ESA), raising concerns among environmental advocates. The first policy rescinds the 'blanket rule' fro...

item.title: Government declares holidays for local anniversary celebrations
item.description: The Philippine government has announced special non-working days in five regions to commemorate local anniversaries and cultural festivals. Proclamation 1349 designates July 20 as a holiday in Labanga...

item.title: Waymo Resumes San Francisco Robotaxi Service After Temporary Pause Due to Power Outage
item.description: Waymo, the Alphabet-owned autonomous vehicle company, temporarily paused its robotaxi service in San Francisco for approximately one hour due to a power outage affecting around 7,000 PG&E customers. T...

item.title: Argentina and England Clash in Nations Championship Rugby Match
item.description: The article details the intense rugby match between Argentina and England in the Nations Championship, highlighting the historical rivalry between the two nations. It mentions the previous football ri...

item.title: Seven U.S. Aid Workers in Quarantine After Ebola Exposure in Congo
item.description: Seven American aid workers have been placed in quarantine at a U.S.-run facility in Kenya following potential exposure to the Ebola virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo, according to Samaritan’s ...
